title: Dasymaschalon longiusculum
taxonomy: Dasymaschalon longiusculum is classified as a species of plant in the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the custard apple family. It belongs to the genus Dasymaschalon, which is a group of flowering plants that are native to Southeast Asia. This plant is a type of evergreen shrub or small tree that produces small, white flowers and red or purple fruit. The exact classification of Dasymaschalon longiusculum within the family Annonaceae may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered to be a member of the order Magnoliales. Further classification of this plant may be subject to ongoing research and may be refined as new information becomes available.

intro: Dasymaschalon longiusculum, also known as the Taiwanese alangium or Chinese allspice, is a plant species in the Annonaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of D. longiusculum include lignans, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
